definition: Edible food as the physical nutriment sustaining embodied life.
notes: The project keeps edible-food nutriment as the default because it stays transparent to the Pāli compound while distinguishing this nutriment from the subtler three. Physical nutriment remains a controlled alternate when English needs a smoother phrase, but the term should not collapse into generic food-talk or materialism language. This entry needs to stay coordinated with āhāra as the broad category and with the other three nutriments as one member of the same doctrinal set. The OSF glossary supports keeping kabaḷīkārāhāra explicit inside the four-nutriments framework.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose emphasizing bodily sustenance
context_rules.notes: Allow this as a controlled alternate only when the context clearly supports it.

context_rules.context: When contrasting this nutriment with contact, mental intention, and knowing
context_rules.notes: Keep the specifically edible and bodily side explicit.

example_phrases.notes: One member of the four nutriments.

authority_basis.scope: Named in notes as support for the current kabaḷīkārāhāra policy.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'edible-food nutriment' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Yield to recorded context_rules, source-facing technical use, or other explicitly documented local reasons rather than rotating among alternates ad hoc.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift back toward 'materialism' or other unrecorded alternates.
